rules.description: Captures names appearing after 'Beschwerdesache', 'Strafsache', 'Rechtssache', or 'gegen' (against) in legal contexts, ensuring full name capture. Handles standalone surnames and multi-word names.

rules.name: Person after 'geboren am'
rules.description: Captures the name of a person appearing after 'geboren am' (born on) in legal texts. Excludes month names.

rules.name: Person after 'gegen' (Criminal Context)
rules.description: Captures names appearing after 'gegen' (against) in criminal contexts, ensuring full name capture including titles and excluding company names. Handles standalone surnames.

rules.name: Person after 'Angeklagten' or 'Angeklagte'
rules.description: Captures names of defendants appearing after 'Angeklagten' (accused) or 'Angeklagte' in legal texts.

rules.name: Person after 'Zeugin' or 'Zeuge'
rules.description: Captures names of witnesses appearing after 'Zeugin' or 'Zeuge'.

rules.name: Person with Complex Academic Titles
rules.description: Captures full names including academic titles, strictly excluding company suffixes (GmbH, AG, etc.) immediately following the name.

rules.name: Person after 'Partei' (Plaintiff/Defendant)
rules.description: Captures person names immediately following 'Partei', explicitly handling multiple academic titles, suffixes, and special characters (umlauts, accents, hyphens). Excludes company names using strict negative lookahead.

rules.name: Person after 'vertreten durch' (Refined)
rules.description: Captures names of legal representatives following 'vertreten durch', strictly handling titles and excluding law firm names using negative lookahead.

rules.name: Person after Company Name in List
rules.description: Captures person names appearing immediately after a company name (ending in GmbH, AG, etc.) in a list context, often followed by a comma. Excludes law firm names and city names.

rules.name: Surname after 'gegen' (Criminal Context)
rules.description: Captures the surname of a person appearing after 'gegen' (against) in criminal contexts, specifically targeting the last word of the name if a full name is present, or the single name if standalone.

rules.name: Surname after 'Angeklagten' or 'Angeklagte'
rules.description: Captures the surname of a defendant appearing after 'Angeklagten' (accused) or 'Angeklagte' in legal texts.

rules.name: Standalone Surname in Civil Context
rules.description: Captures standalone surnames in civil contexts, often appearing after 'Partei' if not a company, or in lists. Excludes legal nouns, common words, and city names.

rules.name: Standalone Surname in Criminal Context
rules.description: Captures standalone surnames in criminal contexts, specifically targeting the surname following 'des', 'der', 'in der Sache', or 'gegen' when the full name is not required or when the context implies a surname only.

rules.name: Person after 'Herrn' or 'Frau'
rules.description: Captures names preceded by 'Herrn' or 'Frau' in legal texts, ensuring a name follows the title.

rules.name: Person after 'gegen' (Contextual)
rules.description: Captures names appearing after 'gegen' (against) or 'von' (of/from) in legal contexts, ensuring full name capture including titles and prepositions.

rules.name: Person after 'gegen' or 'von' (Contextual)
